signals.headline: HEADLINE: Goods Inflation Expected to Peak Q1 2026; Tariffs Main Driver, Not Overheating | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
HEADLINE: Goods Inflation Expected to Peak Q1 2026; Tariffs Main Driver, Not Overheating | YES | HIGH confidence**

Powell confirmed goods inflation should peak around Q1 2026 (which has now passed), with the bulk of remaining inflation being "one-time price increases" from tariffs rather than domestic demand overheating.[1] Core inflation excluding tariff-affected goods is "in the low 2%."
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?
